summary: Photo effect preview crashes with a native SIGSEGV (null pointer dereference) when the GL surface is torn down mid-render on the head unit
description: On the AAOS head unit, com.example.app terminates with a native crash whenever the live photo-effect preview is interrupted while a frame is still being rendered - for example when the display is hot-unplugged/re-docked, or the app is backgrounded and its GLSurfaceView is recreated, while a filter pass is in flight. The app's native imaging library (libimagefx.so, which statically links the GPU image-filter engine) faults with signal 11 (SIGSEGV) on the GL render thread 'gl-render'. fault addr is 0x0 (a null pointer dereference). The tombstone points at the shared filter render entry point: CGEImageFilterInterface::render2Texture, reached from CGEBrightnessFilter::render2Texture in the brightness-adjust pass. The first thing render2Texture does is call the target image handler's setAsTarget() to bind the output framebuffer, and the handler pointer passed in is null - the render thread keeps driving the effect pipeline for one more frame after the image handler has been released on the main thread, so a null handler reaches render2Texture and the virtual setAsTarget() call dereferences it. Preview goes black and the activity is force-finished. Reproducible by rapidly toggling the preview surface (dock/undock, or fast app switch) with a brightness filter active; no repro when the surface stays stable. See the attached tombstone.
